negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Wallet fee calculation for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE does not use http_executor_fee(input_len, 20); it uses the fixed DKMS executor fee and dkms_escrow_lockup.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs defines DKMS_OUTPUT_SIZE_BYTES = 20 but calculate_dkms_fees uses dkms_executor_fee() from DKMS_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI.
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/wallet.rs returns verify_fixed_fee_wallet_lock with dkms_escrow_lockup(input_len) for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE.
crates/ritual-precompile-codecs/src/dkms_key.rs provides the DKMS ABI codec but no fee calculation.

notes: This older HTTP-style DKMS pricing invariant is superseded by the fixed-fee DKMS behavior present in the port.
rationale: The merge keeps the dev-side DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE branch in wallet pricing and adds a DKMS key precompile codec module in the same primary merge. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Wallet fee calculation for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE must use HTTP-style executor fee pricing based on input length and a fixed 20-byte output size.
suggested_falsification_tests: Validate a DKMS key async request and assert required fee/lockup equals http_executor_fee(input_len, 20), and that the DKMS codec round-trips the request format used by that precompile.
summary: DKMS wallet pricing is fixed-fee, not HTTP-style.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Validate a DKMS key async request and assert required fee/lockup equals http_executor_fee(input_len, 20), and that the DKMS codec round-trips the request format used by that precompile.
title: DKMS key precompile uses HTTP-style executor fee
